mnemonics: Powers Predict Paths: Raising a stochastic matrix to a power (P^n) gives the probabilities of transitioning from state i to state j in exactly n steps.
keyPoints: A stochastic matrix is a square matrix where each row sums to 1, representing probabilities.
The (i, j)th entry of the matrix represents the probability of moving from state i to state j.
The long-term behavior of a system can be found by analyzing the steady-state vector, which is the left eigenvector corresponding to the eigenvalue 1.
commonMistakes: Assuming the matrix must be symmetric; rows must sum to 1, but columns do not.
Confusing the state transition diagram with the matrix; the matrix is the numerical representation of the diagram's probabilities.
